frameworks.name: heart rate variability as executive function index
frameworks.one_line: HRV taken to reflect prefrontal executive control via cortical-autonomic coupling

frameworks.name: executive dysfunction account of emotional disorders
frameworks.one_line: Depression and anxiety framed as disorders involving impaired executive function

frameworks.name: EEG band-power task reactivity
frameworks.one_line: Working-memory engagement read from task-vs-rest changes in spectral band power

core_question: Does resting HRV correlate with EEG activity during a working memory task in patients with depressive or anxiety disorder?
key_assumptions: HRV indexes executive function and related neural activity
Working memory task engages executive circuits visible in EEG band power
Depression and anxiety share an executive-dysfunction substrate
manipulations: State contrast: resting state vs working memory task
Group contrast: depressive disorder vs anxiety disorder
EEG frequency bands (delta, theta, beta) and scalp region
key_findings: Both groups showed weaker beta relative power during the task than at rest
Resting EEG did not correlate with HRV parameters in either group
In depression, HRV correlated positively with task delta power and negatively with task beta relative power
In anxiety, HRV correlated positively with right frontal theta power during the task
interpretations: HRV-EEG coupling emerges during cognitive engagement and dissociates depression from anxiety in spectral signature
